description_long: This object consists of two parallel arrays of solar panels mounted on a flat, rectangular surface, likely representing a section of ground or a rooftop. Each array is composed of multiple blue photovoltaic modules framed in metallic structures, supported by a visible truss framework beneath. The panels are tilted at a consistent angle, optimizing exposure to sunlight, and are elevated above the base by sturdy, triangular supports. The metallic framework appears to be made of aluminum or steel, providing both stability and durability. No visible electrical inverters or cabling are present, and the structure is designed for stationary installation in an open, unobstructed area. The overall design is typical for ground-mounted solar energy infrastructure found in urban or suburban environments.
description: Two parallel arrays of blue solar panels are mounted on metallic truss structures, elevated and tilted for optimal sunlight capture. The setup is robust and stationary, suitable for ground installation in urban settings.
description_view_0: Top-down view showing two parallel rows of blue solar panels on a gray base, highlighting the panel arrangement and spacing.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the tilted angle of the solar panels and the height of the supporting truss structures.
description_view_2: Bottom view displaying the metallic truss framework supporting the solar panels, with no visible panels from this angle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view again showing the tilt of the panels and the elevation above the base, similar to view 1.
attribute_panel_orientation: tilted at approximately 30 degrees, orientation appears fixed
attribute_mounting_structure_type: ground-mounted with aluminum or steel truss frame
attribute_array_configuration: two parallel linear rows, each with multiple panels arranged side by side
